title: Hett family
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:Hett-Outlander.jpg|thumb|Sharad Hett before joining the [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Sand People]].]]

A family of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] living among [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Sand People]], the '''Hett family''' consisted of exiled [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Sharad Hett]], his wife [[K'Sheek]], and their son [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]].

==History==
K'Sheek was adopted into a [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] tribe at a very young age. Initially, she was a slave but would soon be regarded as one of their own by the Sand People and became a great warrior.

[[File:Asharad.jpg|thumb|left|Powerful [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]].]]

Powerful [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] Sharad Hett, haunted by a feeling of emptiness and self-doubt, was granted leave of absence so that he could rejoin his family, only to learn that his entire family had been killed in a planetary bombardment of his homeworld by off world rival forces. Devastated, Sharad roamed the worlds of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and eventually crash-landed in the deserts of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] where he lived as a wild man until he was captured by the Sand People. He impressed them with his capabilities and eventually was accepted into their ranks as a Tusken warrior. He would end up the leader of a coalition of Tusken tribes.

He and K'Sheek fell in love, and she gave birth to their son A'Sharad Hett. While A'Sharad was still a baby she was lost in a sandstorm and presumably died. Sharad took it upon himself to raise his son and train him in the ways of the Jedi. Fifteen years after A'Sharad's birth, [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]] traveled to Tatooine in search of Sharad Hett. Little did he know that psychotic [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]] was on his tail. During a fierce battle between [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla the Elder]] and [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] combined forces and the coalition of Tusken tribes, Aurra Sing took advantage of the chaos and killed Sharad. The entire coalition of Tusken tribes being wiped out by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] and his father dead, A'Sharad agreed to go with Ki-Adi-Mundi to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and join the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].

One of the few Jedi to survive [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] and the [[Great Jedi Purge]], A'Sharad later returned to Tatooine and his former life as a Sand Person, again assuming leadership of a tribe of Tusken Raiders. While leading a raid on the [[Lars family/Legends|Lars family]] [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farm]], he was intercepted by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who led him to believe he was there to rein him in. A [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] duel broke out, and in typical style Obi-Wan cut A'Sharad's arm off, bringing dishonor to him among the tribe and resulting in the Tusken people abandoning him.

A'Sharad would later learn that Obi-Wan's real reason for being there had been to protect the son of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]].

A'Sharad later turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the force]], becoming a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and taking the name ''Darth Krayt''.
